# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.

Prevent kafka topic authorization.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

# server.properties file configuration 
log4j.logger.org.apache.kafka=INFO, console   # Logging to the stdout/console is enabled for Kafka logs only in INFO level and disabled by default (to prevent kafka topic authorization)
listeners: PLAINTEXT://localhost:9092  # Listen on localhost port 9092, change as per requirement. This will be the broker's address for clients to connect with Kafka brokers in a cluster environment  
advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your_broker_address:9093    # The advertised listeners are used by producers and consumers, they listen on this port (default is 9092) but can be changed to any available unoccupied TCP/IP port
zookeeper.connect=localhost:2181   # Zookeeper connection string for the Kafka cluster in a distributed environment like our case with two brokers, it's localhost and default zk server (default is 2181) but can be changed to any available unoccupied TCP/IP port
broker.id=0   # Broker ID set by user for the Kafka cluster in a distributed environment like our case with two brokers, it's unique per broker and default value (default is 0). It should match between all nodes of your kafka setup to ensure uniqueness across different clusters
log.dir=/tmp/kafka-logs   # Log directory for Kafka where logs are stored by the brokers, it'll be a folder on disk and can change as per requirement (default is /tmp) but should match with log4j configuration to ensure correct logging location 
num.replicas=1    # Number of replicas set in Zookeeper for each Kafka topic/partition under the same partitioned group, it's used by zk watcher and replication controller (default is 1) but can be changed as per requirement
